seoTitle: How to Log Out of All Devices on PayPal (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to force a sign-out across every device linked to your PayPal account to protect your wallet after using shared or old hardware.
intro: Leaving your PayPal account active on an old phone or shared computer can put your funds and linked cards at risk. This guide shows how to revoke every remembered session at once through PayPal's security settings. It's especially useful after losing a device or using a public computer.

steps.heading: Open Security settings
steps.body: Log in to PayPal, click the Settings gear icon, and select the Security tab.

steps.heading: Find Manage Your Logins
steps.body: Scroll down to locate the Manage Your Logins or Auto Login option.

steps.heading: Open the device list
steps.body: Click Manage to see the full list of remembered browsers and devices tied to your account.

steps.heading: Log out of all devices
steps.body: Select the option to log out of all devices, then confirm the action on the overlay that appears.

steps.heading: Verify the change
steps.body: Refresh your settings feed to confirm that every session except your current browser has been revoked.

faq.question: Will this log me out of my current session too?
faq.answer: No, the global log-out typically keeps your current active browser signed in while closing every other session.

faq.question: Do I need to change my password afterward?
faq.answer: It's a good idea to reset your password as well if you suspect unauthorized access, in addition to logging out remotely.

faq.question: How often should I check my logged-in devices?
faq.answer: Periodically reviewing this list, especially after using shared computers, helps keep your account secure.

faq.question: Will I need to log in again on my own trusted devices?
faq.answer: Yes, any device you use afterward will need to sign in again and may go through standard security verification.
